title: Energy Consumption and Efficiency by Facility
exclusions: Does not compute GHG Protocol Scope 1–3 tonnes CO2e, dual Scope 2 location-versus-market grids, or jurisdiction-specific carbon factor stewardship (see UC-23.9.1). Does not replace certified energy audits, investment-grade metering submittals, or utility bill reconciliation for tariff settlement — it is an operational energy-intensity and attribution layer on BMS-grade sub-meter telemetry.
dataSources: Primary: sub-meter kilowatt-hour telemetry ingested through Splunk HTTP Event Collector into `index=facilities` with `sourcetype="power_meter"`, carrying at minimum `site` (or `facility`), `meter_id`, `system` (branch or load name such as CH-01, LIGHT-L2, IT-PDU-A), `_time`, and one of `kwh_delta`, `kwh_total`, `kwh`, or `kwh_consumed` per poll (BACnet/IP AI objects with engineering-units property 19 kilowatt-hours from Johnson Controls Metasys, Siemens Desigo CC, Schneider EcoStruxure Building, Honeywell Niagara, Trane Tracer SC+, Distech EC-Net; Modbus TCP holding-register pairs from Schneider PowerLogic ION8650 / Veris H8035 class meters). Supplementary dimensional context via CSV lookup `lookups/facility_details.csv` (deploy as `facility_details.csv`) keyed on `site` returning `square_metres`, `occupancy`, `climate_zone` (ASHRAE climate zone string such as 6A Minneapolis versus 2B Phoenix) so Splunk can compute kilowatt-hours per square metre Energy Use Intensity and compare sites on equal footing before weather-adjustment overlays land in later iterations.
app: Splunk HTTP Event Collector (ships with Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud; Settings → Data inputs → HTTP Event Collector — no separate Splunkbase ID) for JSON payloads from BMS gateways or IoT collectors; Splunk DB Connect (`splunk_app_db_connect`, Splunkbase 2686) to hydrate `facility_details.csv` from a facilities master table (space inventory, occupancy headcount, climate zone). Optional Splunk Edge Hub when field protocol conversion (Modbus TCP/RTU, BACnet MS/TP behind a serial concentrator) happens at the edge before HEC; optional Splunk Connect for SNMP when power meters expose kWh counters over SNMP instead of BACnet/Modbus.
spl:
index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
| eval system=trim(coalesce(system,meter_loop,"UNATTRIBUTED"))
| eval kwh_val=tonumber(coalesce(kwh_delta,kwh_total,kwh,kwh_consumed,"0"))
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(kwh_val) as daily_kwh by site, system, _time
| eval date_wday=strftime(_time,"%A")
| eval day_kind=case(lower(date_wday) IN ("saturday","sunday"), "weekend", 1=1, "weekday")
| eval sys_att=case(
    match(upper(system),"HVAC|CHILL|BOILER|AHU|RTU|VAV|FCU|PUMP|COOL|CHW"), "HVAC",
    match(upper(system),"LIGHT|LGT|FIXTURE|LED"), "lighting",
    match(upper(system),"IT|UPS|PDU|SERVER|DATA|NETWORK|MDF|IDF"), "IT_load",
    match(upper(system),"PLUG|RECEPT|EV|SHED"), "plug_load",
    1=1, "other")
| stats sum(daily_kwh) as period_kwh by site, sys_att, day_kind
| lookup facility_details.csv site OUTPUT square_metres, occupancy, climate_zone
| eval eui=if(square_metres>0, round(period_kwh/square_metres, 4), null())
| eventstats avg(eui) as portfolio_eui by sys_att, day_kind
| eval pct_vs_portfolio=if(portfolio_eui>0, round(100*(eui-portfolio_eui)/portfolio_eui, 2), null())
| join type=left site [
    search index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-365d@d latest=-335d@d
    | eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
    | eval kwh_val=tonumber(coalesce(kwh_delta,kwh_total,kwh,kwh_consumed,"0"))
    | bin _time span=1d
    | stats sum(kwh_val) as base_kwh by site
    | lookup facility_details.csv site OUTPUT square_metres as sq_m
    | eval baseline_eui=if(sq_m>0, round(base_kwh/sq_m, 4), null())
    | fields site, baseline_eui
  ]
| eval yoy_eui_delta=if(isnotnull(baseline_eui), round(eui-baseline_eui, 4), null())
| sort site, sys_att, day_kind
| table site, sys_att, day_kind, climate_zone, square_metres, occupancy, period_kwh, eui, baseline_eui, yoy_eui_delta, portfolio_eui, pct_vs_portfolio
description: Rolls up ninety-day facility power-meter telemetry into daily buckets, attributes each branch load to HVAC, lighting, IT, plug-load, or other buckets, joins square-metres and climate zone from `facility_details.csv`, computes Energy Use Intensity as kilowatt-hours per square metre, contrasts weekday versus weekend consumption for wasted-energy screening, compares each slice against a fleet-average portfolio curve, and joins a prior-year baseline window so YoY EUI deltas surface before facilities leadership interprets savings programmes.
value: Facilities directors and regional energy engineers gain a single pane that ranks sites by intensity, not by raw megawatt-hours, and decomposes where kilowatts go across mechanical versus lighting versus IT loads — the narrative utilities cannot supply from one revenue-meter bill. Portfolio comparisons expose outliers justified by climate zone versus genuinely leaky schedules (weekend HVAC overrides, orphaned lighting circuits). YoY deltas anchored on the same calendar slice temper slogans about efficiency projects with meter-grounded proof ahead of capex committees and landlord negotiations without leaning on unrelated greenhouse-gas ledger mechanics.
implementation: Publish HEC tokens for BMS JSON into `facilities`; normalize `system` strings upstream so attribution regex stays stable; sync `facility_details.csv` from IWMS or Snowflake via DB Connect nightly; deploy the canonical SPL as a scheduled saved search feeding Dashboard Studio boards with tokens for trailing ninety versus baseline slice; alert when weekend HVAC kilowatt-hours exceed weekday HVAC intensity after occupancy normalization for a site with high `unattributed` share — wire to facilities inbox first, not ESG disclosure owners.
visualization: Dashboard Studio layout: trellis column chart of `eui` by `site` split `sys_att` (HVAC, lighting, IT_load, plug_load, other); overlay line series of `portfolio_eui` as reference; paired small-multiple single values for `yoy_eui_delta` on the two noisiest sites; heatmap `day_kind` × `sys_att` for weekend waste; drilldown table matching the closing `table` command with colour rules when `pct_vs_portfolio` exceeds fifteen percent or `yoy_eui_delta` remains positive despite retrofit badges.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector
ENERGY STAR Portfolio Manager technical reference — illustrative median office site EUI 64 kBtu/ft² (≈6.7 kWh/m² basis varies by gross vs conditioned floor-area conventions)
ANSI/ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1 (Energy Standard for Buildings) — overview and EUI benchmarking context
ANSI/ASHRAE Standard 135-2020 — BACnet: A Data Communication Protocol for Building Automation and Control Networks

knownFalsePositives: A cold January in Toronto can push the same metered kilowatt-hours as a July heat wave in Phoenix through entirely different HVAC drivers — heating-degree-day versus cooling-degree-day dominance means EUI rises in both cities without a sudden efficiency collapse, so treat raw kilowatt-hours per square metre spikes next to seasonal degree-day overlays before blaming controls. Hybrid return-to-office shifts headcount without floor-area changes: energy per occupant swings while kilowatt-hours per square metre stays flat, so workforce analytics can scream waste when the building simply hosts fewer desks. Large unattributed buckets appear when only the main switchboard is metered but lighting circuits remain estimated from breaker labels — Splunk faithfully shows other above forty percent while the gap is instrumentation, not mystery load. Demand-response programmes (ERCOT emergency response service, CAISO capacity bids, PJM economic load response) legitimately shed one hundred to five hundred kilowatts for hours — EUI drops are compliance with grid stress, not proof of deeper savings. Electric-vehicle chargers behind plug-load meters add fifty to two hundred kilowatt-hours per day as a deliberate sustainability investment; weekend curves change without inefficiency. Multi-tenant sites see step-changes when a restaurant kitchen or colocation cage energises — intensity jumps with no chiller fault. Current-transformer clamps drift three to eight percent high after five tropical years without recalibration, inflating EUI until maintenance replaces CTs or applies correction factors.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.2 or newer or Splunk Cloud; role grants `index=facilities` plus read access to `facility_details` lookup definitions on search heads used by facilities engineering and sustainability-adjacent analysts who do not own UC-23.9.1 ledgers.
• HTTP Event Collector global settings enabled; tokens minted per data path (BMS gateway versus edge forwarder) with acknowledgement if the publisher can honour `X-Splunk-Request-Channel`.
• Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) on a Heavy Forwarder with JDBC connectivity to the integrated workplace management system or data warehouse table that exports `site`, `square_metres`, `occupancy`, `climate_zone` — column names must map to the lookup headers expected in Step 1.
• CSV `facility_details.csv` uploaded under Lookups → Lookup table files with a matching transforms.conf stanza named `facility_details`; alternatively mirror the same fields into KV Store when multiple authors need row-level ACLs.
• Governance note locking how `system` tags are minted upstream (BACnet object name versus Modbus map) so HVAC versus lighting attribution rules stay comparable quarter to quarter.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Route BACnet/IP (default UDP 47808 to `0xBAC0`) or Modbus TCP (port 502, function code 03 read holding registers) gateway output through a collector that emits JSON events with stable `site` keys and engineering-unit-normalised kilowatt-hour increments — prefer delta pulses per interval when the meter exposes both register total and difference; if only cumulative `kwh_total` exists, compute deltas upstream to avoid double counting on poll jitter. Configure HEC to land in `index=facilities` with `sourcetype=power_meter` and map vendor timestamps into `_time` using the meter clock, not indexer arrival, for day-boundary fidelity when comparing to utility billing windows. For SNMP-only meters (racked PDUs), deploy Splunk Connect for SNMP with poll intervals aligned to billing teams’ expectations; flag agents that poll five seconds versus BACnet sixty seconds so you do not blend incompatible interpolation policies in the same summary index. Maintain `facility_details.csv` either by scheduled DB Connect output (`cron_schedule` aligned to workplace occupancy surveys) or by Git-versioned CSV upload; include `climate_zone` so analysts can overlay heating-degree-day and cooling-degree-day feeds later without re-keying manual spreadsheets. Space areas must follow the same gross floor area definition your lease abstracts use — mixed-use towers sometimes store rentable square feet while engineering stores gross; pick one column and rename it consistently in the lookup header to `square_metres` with explicit unit discipline even if upstream data is still in imperial (apply a fixed 0.092903 conversion in the DB SQL once, not inside every ad-hoc search).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the authoritative SPL from the `spl` field into a saved report `fac_energy_eui_attribution_v1` owned by the facilities analytics team. Schedule a daily run over `earliest=-90d@d latest=@d` for dashboard consumption, and clone a second saved search limited to trailing thirty days when leadership wants a sharper Ops zoom. Wire alerts sparingly: example predicate flags sites where `sys_att=HVAC` and `day_kind=weekend` yields `period_kwh` greater than half of the paired weekday HVAC slice while occupancy lookup shows below twenty-five percent nominal — throttle by `site` for seven days because transient festivals or campus reunions legitimately animate weekends.

```spl
index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
| eval system=trim(coalesce(system,meter_loop,"UNATTRIBUTED"))
| eval kwh_val=tonumber(coalesce(kwh_delta,kwh_total,kwh,kwh_consumed,"0"))
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(kwh_val) as daily_kwh by site, system, _time
| eval date_wday=strftime(_time,"%A")
| eval day_kind=case(lower(date_wday) IN ("saturday","sunday"), "weekend", 1=1, "weekday")
| eval sys_att=case(
    match(upper(system),"HVAC|CHILL|BOILER|AHU|RTU|VAV|FCU|PUMP|COOL|CHW"), "HVAC",
    match(upper(system),"LIGHT|LGT|FIXTURE|LED"), "lighting",
    match(upper(system),"IT|UPS|PDU|SERVER|DATA|NETWORK|MDF|IDF"), "IT_load",
    match(upper(system),"PLUG|RECEPT|EV|SHED"), "plug_load",
    1=1, "other")
| stats sum(daily_kwh) as period_kwh by site, sys_att, day_kind
| lookup facility_details.csv site OUTPUT square_metres, occupancy, climate_zone
| eval eui=if(square_metres>0, round(period_kwh/square_metres, 4), null())
| eventstats avg(eui) as portfolio_eui by sys_att, day_kind
| eval pct_vs_portfolio=if(portfolio_eui>0, round(100*(eui-portfolio_eui)/portfolio_eui, 2), null())
| join type=left site [
    search index=facilities sourcetype="power_meter" earliest=-365d@d latest=-335d@d
    | eval site=coalesce(site,facility,location,"Unknown")
    | eval kwh_val=tonumber(coalesce(kwh_delta,kwh_total,kwh,kwh_consumed,"0"))
    | bin _time span=1d
    | stats sum(kwh_val) as base_kwh by site
    | lookup facility_details.csv site OUTPUT square_metres as sq_m
    | eval baseline_eui=if(sq_m>0, round(base_kwh/sq_m, 4), null())
    | fields site, baseline_eui
  ]
| eval yoy_eui_delta=if(isnotnull(baseline_eui), round(eui-baseline_eui, 4), null())
| sort site, sys_att, day_kind
| table site, sys_att, day_kind, climate_zone, square_metres, occupancy, period_kwh, eui, baseline_eui, yoy_eui_delta, portfolio_eui, pct_vs_portfolio
```

Step 3 — Pipeline mechanics
The opening search command applies clear time bounds on `index=facilities` with `sourcetype="power_meter"`, then coalesces alternate site keys so orphaned tags still roll up. The `eval` on `kwh_val` prefers interval deltas when present because delta fields avoid differentiating noisy cumulative registers inside Splunk. The `bin _time span=1d` command locks daily buckets; the first `stats` implements the required `sum(kwh_val) as daily_kwh by site, system, _time` grain before attribution collapses dimensions. Weekday versus weekend separation uses `strftime` weekday names and a `case` that labels Saturday and Sunday as weekend while every other day falls through to weekday, matching facilities runbooks that spot schedule drift. The `sys_att` mapping groups raw BMS branch labels into HVAC, lighting, IT_load, plug_load, or other — extend the regular expressions when a site introduces oceanic locales with diacritics in object names. The second `stats` sums daily kilowatt-hours into `period_kwh` at the site, attribution, and `day_kind` grain. The `lookup` pulls `square_metres`, `occupancy`, and `climate_zone` for denominator context; the guarded ratio only divides when floor area is positive. The `eventstats` pass computes `portfolio_eui` as the average EUI across all sites within each attribution and `day_kind` slice so each row carries a fleet reference without hand-maintained constants. User checklist asks for baseline-period comparison via appendcols-style secondary pulls where row cardinality permits it — here `join type=left site` attaches `baseline_eui` replicated across HVAC versus lighting slices without altering attribution grain; analysts running site-collapsed decks only may substitute `appendcols [ search ... earliest=-365d@d latest=-335d@d ... ]` after `stats ... by site`. Closing `sort` and `table` commands format the analytical output for dashboards and CSV export.

Step 4 — Validate
Verify in the EcoStruxure dashboard that each pilot site's monthly kilowatt-hour rollup aligns with Splunk `sum(period_kwh)` for the same calendar month within a few percentage points after time-zone alignment — repeat the same check against the Siemens Desigo CC energy dashboard where that stack owns the authoritative metres. Spot-check that `square_metres` for those sites matches the latest IWMS export used for chargebacks. Spot-check that `square_metres` for those sites matches the latest IWMS export used for chargebacks. Confirm `system` tokens that should map to HVAC still hit the HVAC bucket by running a short search that lists the top twenty raw `system` values for a week; if chilled-water plant tags use a new naming scheme, adjust the `match` clauses before executives trust attribution. For the join output, sanity-check that `baseline_eui` is null only when the site lacked coverage during the baseline slice — investigate upstream ingestion gaps rather than overriding with guesses.

Step 5 — Operational caveats and known false positives
• Heating-degree-day and cooling-degree-day seasonality can mimic inefficiency: Toronto winter HDD near forty-five hundred can lift HVAC kilowatt-hours alongside Phoenix summer CDD near forty-five hundred — identical metre totals trace different physics; overlay NOAA or MetOffice degree-day feeds before commissioning punitive HVAC tuning tickets.
• Post-pandemic hybrid schedules swing occupants per square metre faster than lease renewals update lookup occupancy fields — intensity per occupant rockets while intensity per square metre stays calm; pair this UC with badged turnstile feeds when headcount-sensitive narratives matter.
• Partial sub-meter deployments strand forty-plus percent of energy inside other because lighting circuits ride unmetered assumptions — prioritise metering investments rather than tightening Splunk thresholds until coverage crosses governance-defined completeness.
• Demand-response dispatch during ERCOT conservation appeals or CAISO Flex Alert windows creates real kilowatt-hour reductions that are grid services, not efficiency wins; annotate events with utility OpenADR flags when available so dashboards do not misread programme compliance as waste elimination.
• Meter swap or CT replacement often leaves a twenty-four to seventy-two hour gap in delta pulses — backfill judiciously with `streamstats` and `fillnull` only when facilities engineering signs off on linear interpolation so a missing day is not mistaken for miraculous savings.
• Daylight-saving clock shifts generate twenty-three and twenty-five hour civil days once per region per year — if board packs require normalised daily energy, multiply each bucket by twenty-four divided by measured hours after the `bin` stage; skip this refinement when leadership only needs month-level views.
When troubleshooting data quality, fewer events than the gateway poll interval predicts for a steady load usually indicates HEC throttling, token mis-scoping to the wrong index, or forwarder clock skew — reconcile `_indextime` minus `_time` before replacing hardware. Missing fields immediately after a lookup upload typically mean transforms.conf column names drifted from the CSV header row — reload lookups and compare field names under Settings.
grandmaExplanation: We look at each building’s electricity use against its floor size so we know which ones are heavy for what they are. We split that use into air-and-heat, lights, computers, and everyday plugs, and compare quiet weekends to busy weekdays so people can see where schedules waste power without turning it into a carbon accounting lecture.
